title: Oberna procumbens
taxonomy: Oberna procumbens is a species of plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It belongs to the genus Oberna which is a group of flowering plants. The plant is further classified as being part of the order Lamiales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species. Oberna procumbens is also known to be closely related to other species within the same family and genus.
uses: Oberna procumbens is traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits. Oberna procumbens has been reported to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various ailments. It is said to have antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating infections. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever and other related conditions. However, further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for medicinal use.

intro: Oberna procumbens, also known as African sage, is a plant species in the family Plumbaginaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oberna procumbens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Oberna procumbens include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
